programme.desc: The start of the new school term leads to a spate of stabbings and a forester is badly injured in an accident involving a chainsaw deep in woods near Keighley

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker as DI Annika Strandhed, who has returned to Glasgow to head up the Marine Homicide Unit, where she is tasked with solving puzzling crimes and unexplained murders that wash up in Scotlands waters. On her first day in a new role, Annika is called in to solve the murder of a man found in the Clyde, with a harpoon piercing his head

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: When a body is found on a boat in the Clyde, the case takes the team to the Isle of Bute. Evidence points to a jilted lover, but a darker secret emerges as the team investigate. Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker, Jamie Sives and Katie Leung

programme.title: Emergency: First Time Medics
programme.desc: Follow real-time shifts of newly-qualified doctors at the Royal Berkshire Hospital in Reading. Its induction day for 26 junior doctors as they head to the Emergency Department

programme.title: Katie Piper: Locked Up in Louisiana
programme.desc: Documentary series where the TV presenter uncovers what life is like for women behind bars in the United States at the New Orleans Parish Prison. As she meets the inmates, Katie delves into inmates cases and tries to uncover what drove these women to commit the crimes that they have been accused of.

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Three Swedish Death Cleaners - an organizer, a designer and a psychologist - travel across America to help people face mortality and organise their homes and lives before its too late. The trio help Suzi, a sassy 75-year-old woman surrounded by phallic souvenirs from her travels, let go of the past and move forward

programme.title: Deepfake Porn: Could You Be Next?
programme.desc: The rising trend of harmless images being turned into hardcore porn, footage that is often used against the people depicted with devastating consequences. The film features three women who have been impacted by this form of image-based sexual abuse, including a leading Florida State Senator, Lauren Book, the UK campaigner Kate Isaacs, and Dina, a woman who was deep-faked by a work colleague

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Hopesay, Shropshire to meet ex-fireman Joe and his seven-year-old daughter Lucy. Joe lost his wife Jess suddenly in 2014, partway through renovating an old cottage into what they had hoped would be a dream family home. Now work has ground to a halt and Joe and his daughter are living in a tiny caravan in the garden. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team arrives to give them the home Jess had wanted for them, backed up by an army of local tradespeople

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team help Scott Jones, a 17-year-old disabled athlete in Cheltenham, who is eager to become more self-sufficient at home, with the challenge including added side and rear extensions, a wet room, access to an adapted kitchen and a state-of-the-art gym studio. Scott won gold at the 2013 IPC world championships and aims to develop his talents in order to participate at the 2020 Paralympics in Tokyo

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Derby to meet Rachel and Andy Smith, whose middle child Isaac has a severe form of cerebral palsy. The couple must be available at all times because Isaac sleeps in a hospital bed in the living room, but this is making life difficult for his siblings as their home design is in conflict with what the family really need. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team arrives to help, backed up by an army of local tradespeople - with designer Julia Kendall making sure they do not stray too far from her vision

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: Midwife Linda Abbott comes out of retirement to return to Leeds General Infirmary, while two fathers with very different attitudes await the arrival of their newborns. Joel dotes on wife Rachel and the couple grow more anxious with each day on the labour ward, having lost their first son who was born prematurely. Meanwhile, Chris admits he doesnt have a clue what to do in his role as birth partner, and his attempt to use humour to deal with his nerves fails to go down well in the delivery room

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: The midwives at Leeds General Infirmary deal with a September baby boom and have to send some of the expectant mothers to St Jamess Hospital across the city, which means a last-minute change to birth plans for those affected, like first-time mum Rachel. Natalie and Shane also have to be transferred to a different maternity unit, while Kate and David experience a long wait for her Caesarean due to the number of emergency cases going to theatre

programme.desc: The couple have their first lovers tiff and Gavin is so beside himself with worry he drives all the way to Barry Island to resolve things. programme.title: Emergency: First Time Medics
programme.desc: Junior doctor Alex handles a paracetamol overdose and a high-risk heart patient, Adnan braves his first shift in Resus, and Claire deals with a woman in agonising stomach pain

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Shana knows she can be uptight, but talking to friends about a terminal cancer diagnosis is daunting and the Death Cleaners help with her fear of discussing the end

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis interviews some of the biggest names in UK entertainment and is granted exclusive access into their lives at home and at work, beginning with platinum-selling rapper Stormzy. After two years out of the spotlight, Stormzy welcomes Louis into his home in south-west London to sit down for a frank and revealing conversation that covers many aspects of his life; from his childhood to the breakdown of a high-profile relationship, and the pressures that come with being one of the countrys best-loved musicians and role models

programme.title: Tipping Point

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a jackpot of up to £20,000

programme.title: Katie Piper: Locked Up in Louisiana
programme.desc: Documentary series where the TV presenter uncovers what life is like for women behind bars in the United States at the New Orleans Parish Prison. As she meets the inmates, Katie delves into inmates cases and tries to uncover what drove these women to commit the crimes that they have been accused of.

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker looks back on his documentaries about the criminal justice system in the US. Louis reflects on his reports on the prison system, which exposed a world with its own rules and codes and a system that seemed to be broken. He also revisits the documentaries he shot in Philadelphia and Milwaukee, where tensions between the African-American community and the police were already at boiling point years before the death of George Floyd

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker looks back on his documentaries focused on families, including his trilogy of documentaries centred around the Westboro Baptist Church. He examines how the death of patriarch Fred Phelps has left members of the church divided. He also reflects on how physical and mental health or forces beyond control can threaten to derail family lives

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS: Homes for Veterans - Part One
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team take on their most ambitious project to date and transform a whole street of run-down empty properties in Manchester into homes for veterans. They are joined by the local community, as well as Prince William and Prince Harry, who volunteer their assistance on site. In the first edition of the two-part special, the team builds houses for two former soldiers who fought in Iraq and Afghanistan and are suffering from post traumatic stress disorder. However, with the number of volunteers lacking and the buildings proving to be trickier than expected, the build is becoming the toughest yet

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS: Homes for Veterans - Part Two
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team continue with their most ambitious project to date, with the assistance of the local community and two royal helpers, Prince William and Prince Harry. The second edition sees them create a home for a veteran, who is a triple amputee after an IED blew up in Afghanistan. They are also aiming to renovate the fronts of the houses on the street and put in new utilities, as well as creating a lasting legacy in the form of a support centre, which will help other veterans find homes and jobs

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team heads to Birmingham to transform the family home of parents Charlotte and Chris, whose twins were born prematurely and have a condition known as global development delay. It has slowed down their progress of walking and talking, but they are further hindered by their cramped house. Nick Knowles and the team join forces with a host of volunteers from the local community to rebuild the familys life and give them a better future

programme.desc: Drew Scott and Jonathan Scott help a woman looking for a fresh start to find a modern property as her new home

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: Property experts Drew Scott and Jonathan Scott help couple Dereck and Christine trade in their small urban condo for a roomier, detached home

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The start of the new school term leads to a spate of stabbings and a forester is badly injured in an accident involving a chainsaw deep in woods near Keighley

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker as DI Annika Strandhed, who has returned to Glasgow to head up the Marine Homicide Unit, where she is tasked with solving puzzling crimes and unexplained murders that wash up in Scotlands waters. On her first day in a new role, Annika is called in to solve the murder of a man found in the Clyde, with a harpoon piercing his head

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: When a body is found on a boat in the Clyde, the case takes the team to the Isle of Bute. Evidence points to a jilted lover, but a darker secret emerges as the team investigate. Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker, Jamie Sives and Katie Leung

programme.title: Tortured to Death: Murdering the Nanny
programme.desc: Documentary examining the murder of a young French nanny, 21-year-old Sophie Lionnet, whose body was then burned in the back garden of a south London home. Featuring interviews with friends, neighbours and Old Bailey reporters

programme.title: Emergency: First Time Medics
programme.desc: Junior doctor Alex handles a paracetamol overdose and a high-risk heart patient, Adnan braves his first shift in Resus, and Claire deals with a woman in agonising stomach pain

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Three Swedish Death Cleaners - an organizer, a designer and a psychologist - travel across America to help people face mortality and organise their homes and lives before its too late. The trio help Suzi, a sassy 75-year-old woman surrounded by phallic souvenirs from her travels, let go of the past and move forward

programme.title: Deepfake Porn: Could You Be Next?
programme.desc: The rising trend of harmless images being turned into hardcore porn, footage that is often used against the people depicted with devastating consequences. The film features three women who have been impacted by this form of image-based sexual abuse, including a leading Florida State Senator, Lauren Book, the UK campaigner Kate Isaacs, and Dina, a woman who was deep-faked by a work colleague

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Hopesay, Shropshire to meet ex-fireman Joe and his seven-year-old daughter Lucy. Joe lost his wife Jess suddenly in 2014, partway through renovating an old cottage into what they had hoped would be a dream family home. Now work has ground to a halt and Joe and his daughter are living in a tiny caravan in the garden. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team arrives to give them the home Jess had wanted for them, backed up by an army of local tradespeople

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team help Scott Jones, a 17-year-old disabled athlete in Cheltenham, who is eager to become more self-sufficient at home, with the challenge including added side and rear extensions, a wet room, access to an adapted kitchen and a state-of-the-art gym studio. Scott won gold at the 2013 IPC world championships and aims to develop his talents in order to participate at the 2020 Paralympics in Tokyo

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Derby to meet Rachel and Andy Smith, whose middle child Isaac has a severe form of cerebral palsy. The couple must be available at all times because Isaac sleeps in a hospital bed in the living room, but this is making life difficult for his siblings as their home design is in conflict with what the family really need. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team arrives to help, backed up by an army of local tradespeople - with designer Julia Kendall making sure they do not stray too far from her vision

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: Together with their toddler and a newborn, Denise and Massimo are squeezed into his old bachelor pad condo. But theyre more than ready to settle down in a dream home

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: Jonathan tries to help Jana and Larry, a couple who were in the middle of renovating their dream home when a family crisis moved them back in with parents

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Pete travels to Northern Ireland to pick up three stray dogs and bring them back to Essex. He also meets a Shar Pei who is in constant pain and may need surgery

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: Midwife Linda Abbott comes out of retirement to return to Leeds General Infirmary, while two fathers with very different attitudes await the arrival of their newborns. Joel dotes on wife Rachel and the couple grow more anxious with each day on the labour ward, having lost their first son who was born prematurely. Meanwhile, Chris admits he doesnt have a clue what to do in his role as birth partner, and his attempt to use humour to deal with his nerves fails to go down well in the delivery room

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: The midwives at Leeds General Infirmary deal with a September baby boom and have to send some of the expectant mothers to St Jamess Hospital across the city, which means a last-minute change to birth plans for those affected, like first-time mum Rachel. Natalie and Shane also have to be transferred to a different maternity unit, while Kate and David experience a long wait for her Caesarean due to the number of emergency cases going to theatre

programme.title: Emergency: First Time Medics
programme.desc: Follow real-time shifts of newly-qualified doctors at the Royal Berkshire Hospital in Reading. Its induction day for 26 junior doctors as they head to the Emergency Department

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The couple announce their engagement, prompting the Shipmans to throw a party in Billericay. Even Bryns decision to use his sat-nav for the journey fails to dampen their spirits, and everything goes swimmingly until the conversation turns to money - and the sweethearts drop a bombshell. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: The Death Cleaners help Lindsey after her mother and extended family leave everything they dont want at her house - causing her to lose her personal style

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Shana knows she can be uptight, but talking to friends about a terminal cancer diagnosis is daunting and the Death Cleaners help with her fear of discussing the end

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis interviews some of the biggest names in UK entertainment and is granted exclusive access into their lives at home and at work, beginning with platinum-selling rapper Stormzy. After two years out of the spotlight, Stormzy welcomes Louis into his home in south-west London to sit down for a frank and revealing conversation that covers many aspects of his life; from his childhood to the breakdown of a high-profile relationship, and the pressures that come with being one of the countrys best-loved musicians and role models

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team are on the hunt to restore one of the greatest names in motoring history - the Bugatti. But with a strict budget they settle for a replica

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Austin Healeys are classic British vehicles that are difficult to tame. Paul and Charlie have the rare chance to pick up a trio of these iconic cars in varying conditions

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dereks got his work cut out collecting a huge haul that includes two Bentleys, a Daimler and a traditional Bowtop caravan

programme.title: Ghosts of Alderney: Hitlers Island Slaves
programme.desc: Artist Piers Secunda uncovers the hidden history of Alderney to reveal new evidence of atrocities committed by the Nazis on this tiny island during the Second World War. He examines how slave workers from across Europe were shipped to Alderney to build a fortress for Hitler

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo completes his rail journey through 1930s Britain, beginning in Norfolk at the headquarters of an international horse welfare organisation. In Peterborough, he visits a tidal defence barrier that helped save the city from flooding, while in Lincolnshire crosses the point at which the Mallard broke the speed record for a steam-powered locomotive in 1938. After a spell in the coastal town of Skegness, Michaels journey ends at RAF Coningsby where he sees some iconic Second World War aircraft, before witnessing a dazzling display featuring the RAFs latest combat aircraft

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his 1930s Bradshaws guide, Michael Portillo sets out on another railway adventure as he explores Britain between the world wars. He begins with a tour of the Home Counties and beyond, starting in the city of Oxford, where in 1921 women were awarded degrees for the first time. At Somerville College, Michael finds out about pioneering crystallographer Dorothy Hodgkin, the only British woman to win a Nobel Prize in chemistry

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his travels through Englands Home Counties at Stoke Mandeville in Buckinghamshire, where he discovers the legacy of a German Jewish doctor who fled the Nazis to settle in Britain. He then heads through the Chiltern Hills to Princes Risborough, before reaching Beaconsfield and revisiting his much-loved childhood haunt, Bekonscot Model Village

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to leafy Hatch End in north-west London, where he meets the great-nephew of the famous illustrator William Heath Robinson. Michael continues his journey through Slough and Stoke Poges before reaching Windsor, where he surveys the great walls of the castle, chief residence of the British monarch and the oldest and largest occupied castle in the world

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave has to extricate a VW Golf from a garden that has grown up around it

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek is off to Bridlington to pick up a trio of classics - a Morris 8 tourer, a Mini Van and a 1940s Morris LC3 truck

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Acclaimed chronicle of the Second World War, beginning with an account of Hitlers ascent to power at a time when the Germans were stricken by humiliating military defeat, runaway inflation and economic depression. The Nazi leaders promises of regained national pride and prosperity struck a chord with millions of ordinary citizens, and he was elected on a tidal wave of popular support. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: Crossword puzzles in a national newspaper begin to attract the attention of MI5 when they appear to reveal information about the upcoming D-Day landings

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Bishops Palace in Wells, where finds include a blues guitar, a broken plate by the Victorian designer Augustus Pugin, and a piece of street art on a metal panel. Plus, the story of one of the first female gymnasts to compete in the Olympics in 1928, furniture specialist Lennox Cato discusses his life in antiques, and Fiona is challenged to put three pieces of stunning Faberge jewellery in order of value

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to Ham House in Richmond-upon-Thames, and picture specialist Alexandra Gill talks about how Tutankhamun inspired her journey into antiques. Items brought in to be valued include an 18th-century effigy, an Indian Order of Merit medal awarded to a Sikh soldier in the First World War, an 18th-century pistol and a gold necklace from the Alaskan Gold Rush. Plus, Jon Baddeley challenges Fiona to rank three intriguing optical items in order of value.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A rare Reliant Scimitar GTC undergoes an expert recommission that leaves no bolt unturned, and a Honda CBR superbike from the 1980s needs a repair

programme.desc: The team are on the hunt to restore one of the greatest names in motoring history - the Bugatti. But with a strict budget they settle for a replica

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary in which the presenter takes a tour of the country, travelling from the islands of the Aegean to Athens. Along the way he meets local people including gun-toting priests and the last sponge-divers in the world, and discovers how they are coping with their countrys current crises

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Documentary following the exploits of a family-run crew of heavy-haulage specialists as they transport the heaviest, longest and most precious locomotives around the world. Terry and Jason have been called in to move legendary steam locomotive Britannia. The 68-year-old vehicle has undergone a major restoration in Crewe and is scheduled to start running at the Severn Valley Railway

programme.title: David Jasons Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor examines British innovations in the history of flight, from the first glider to the modern jet suit, and discovers how Britain contributed to the moon landing

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A rare Reliant Scimitar GTC undergoes an expert recommission that leaves no bolt unturned, and a Honda CBR superbike from the 1980s needs a repair

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A 90s Alfa with electrical issues lives up to its stereotype and the team revisit a road roller, four years on. They also tackle a French motoring icon, the Citroen 2CV6 Dolly

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave has to extricate a VW Golf from a garden that has grown up around it

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek is off to Bridlington to pick up a trio of classics - a Morris 8 tourer, a Mini Van and a 1940s Morris LC3 truck

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: An examination of the dark history behind Britains fascination with murder, beginning with historian and curator Lucy Worsley exploring how notorious killings were transformed into popular entertainment in the first half of the 19th century

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo resumes his tour of the Home Counties in the historic town of Guildford. Later, in Chilworth, he follows his 1936 Bradshaws guide to Newlands Corner on the slopes of the North Downs. Eventually he crosses into Hampshire to reach Aldershot

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads for Farnborough, Hampshire, famous today for its airshow and home to what was then the Royal Aircraft Establishment. In Basingstoke he visits a glorious neo-Gothic stately home set in the North Wessex Downs

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores East Anglia between the wars, beginning at Sutton Hoo in Suffolk, where archaeologists discovered a 27-metre-long Anglo-Saxon ship in 1939. In the Essex village of Dedham, he unearths a nasty brush between painters, with art experts explaining the antipathy between traditional artist Alfred Munnings and the modern art school established in the village by Cedric Morris

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his Bradshaws-inspired journey through East Anglia, where he discovers the Essex origins of the BBC. He also joins the Womens Land Army to pick damsons at Tiptree and visits homes fit for heroes in Becontree

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads off to Blackpool to pick up a couple of unusual vehicles - a red 1970 Opel GT and a 1956 Ford Hot Rod pickup

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek meets the owner and builder of a sporty vintage Jaguar special, a Jaguar Viking

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The months following prime minister Neville Chamberlains declaration of war against Germany, in which the only casualties on the home front were those who had to endure the blackouts. Elsewhere in Europe, the Nazi war machine cut a swathe of destruction. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: World War Weird

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dereks got his work cut out collecting a huge haul that includes two Bentleys, a Daimler and a traditional Bowtop caravan

programme.title: Ghosts of Alderney: Hitlers Island Slaves
programme.desc: Artist Piers Secunda uncovers the hidden history of Alderney to reveal new evidence of atrocities committed by the Nazis on this tiny island during the Second World War. He examines how slave workers from across Europe were shipped to Alderney to build a fortress for Hitler

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo completes his rail journey through 1930s Britain, beginning in Norfolk at the headquarters of an international horse welfare organisation. In Peterborough, he visits a tidal defence barrier that helped save the city from flooding, while in Lincolnshire crosses the point at which the Mallard broke the speed record for a steam-powered locomotive in 1938. After a spell in the coastal town of Skegness, Michaels journey ends at RAF Coningsby where he sees some iconic Second World War aircraft, before witnessing a dazzling display featuring the RAFs latest combat aircraft

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his 1930s Bradshaws guide, Michael Portillo sets out on another railway adventure as he explores Britain between the world wars. He begins with a tour of the Home Counties and beyond, starting in the city of Oxford, where in 1921 women were awarded degrees for the first time. At Somerville College, Michael finds out about pioneering crystallographer Dorothy Hodgkin, the only British woman to win a Nobel Prize in chemistry

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his travels through Englands Home Counties at Stoke Mandeville in Buckinghamshire, where he discovers the legacy of a German Jewish doctor who fled the Nazis to settle in Britain. He then heads through the Chiltern Hills to Princes Risborough, before reaching Beaconsfield and revisiting his much-loved childhood haunt, Bekonscot Model Village

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to leafy Hatch End in north-west London, where he meets the great-nephew of the famous illustrator William Heath Robinson. Michael continues his journey through Slough and Stoke Poges before reaching Windsor, where he surveys the great walls of the castle, chief residence of the British monarch and the oldest and largest occupied castle in the world

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave has to extricate a VW Golf from a garden that has grown up around it

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek is off to Bridlington to pick up a trio of classics - a Morris 8 tourer, a Mini Van and a 1940s Morris LC3 truck

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Acclaimed chronicle of the Second World War, beginning with an account of Hitlers ascent to power at a time when the Germans were stricken by humiliating military defeat, runaway inflation and economic depression. The Nazi leaders promises of regained national pride and prosperity struck a chord with millions of ordinary citizens, and he was elected on a tidal wave of popular support. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: Crossword puzzles in a national newspaper begin to attract the attention of MI5 when they appear to reveal information about the upcoming D-Day landings

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Bishops Palace in Wells, where finds include a blues guitar, a broken plate by the Victorian designer Augustus Pugin, and a piece of street art on a metal panel. Plus, the story of one of the first female gymnasts to compete in the Olympics in 1928, furniture specialist Lennox Cato discusses his life in antiques, and Fiona is challenged to put three pieces of stunning Faberge jewellery in order of value

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to Ham House in Richmond-upon-Thames, and picture specialist Alexandra Gill talks about how Tutankhamun inspired her journey into antiques. Items brought in to be valued include an 18th-century effigy, an Indian Order of Merit medal awarded to a Sikh soldier in the First World War, an 18th-century pistol and a gold necklace from the Alaskan Gold Rush. Plus, Jon Baddeley challenges Fiona to rank three intriguing optical items in order of value.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A rare Reliant Scimitar GTC undergoes an expert recommission that leaves no bolt unturned, and a Honda CBR superbike from the 1980s needs a repair

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team are on the hunt to restore one of the greatest names in motoring history - the Bugatti. But with a strict budget they settle for a replica

programme.title: The World at War

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary in which the presenter takes a tour of the country, travelling from the islands of the Aegean to Athens. Along the way he meets local people including gun-toting priests and the last sponge-divers in the world, and discovers how they are coping with their countrys current crises

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Documentary following the exploits of a family-run crew of heavy-haulage specialists as they transport the heaviest, longest and most precious locomotives around the world. Terry and Jason have been called in to move legendary steam locomotive Britannia. The 68-year-old vehicle has undergone a major restoration in Crewe and is scheduled to start running at the Severn Valley Railway

programme.title: David Jasons Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor examines British innovations in the history of flight, from the first glider to the modern jet suit, and discovers how Britain contributed to the moon landing

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo resumes his tour of the Home Counties in the historic town of Guildford. Later, in Chilworth, he follows his 1936 Bradshaws guide to Newlands Corner on the slopes of the North Downs. Eventually he crosses into Hampshire to reach Aldershot

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads for Farnborough, Hampshire, famous today for its airshow and home to what was then the Royal Aircraft Establishment. In Basingstoke he visits a glorious neo-Gothic stately home set in the North Wessex Downs

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores East Anglia between the wars, beginning at Sutton Hoo in Suffolk, where archaeologists discovered a 27-metre-long Anglo-Saxon ship in 1939. In the Essex village of Dedham, he unearths a nasty brush between painters, with art experts explaining the antipathy between traditional artist Alfred Munnings and the modern art school established in the village by Cedric Morris

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his Bradshaws-inspired journey through East Anglia, where he discovers the Essex origins of the BBC. He also joins the Womens Land Army to pick damsons at Tiptree and visits homes fit for heroes in Becontree

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How, in 1940, the French Army suffered a heavy defeat after underestimating the Nazis strength while relying on First World War strategies and equipment to defend its frontiers. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade heads to Thailand in search of the giant freshwater stingray, one of the worlds largest and most terrifying river fish. Armed with a venomous 10in barb on the end of its whip-like tail, this 700lb predator tests the extreme angler to his physical limits, eventually snapping his rod and ripping his bicep

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: An expectant mother attends for treatment, but is harassed by her former husband. Stella is relieved to find she has not contracted an infection from her attacker, but suffers accusations of lying to save her reputation. Meanwhile, Sister Brigid makes a misguided suggestion, and a surprise visitor from overseas forces Goodwin to reconsider his future plans

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the former Crystal Palace and Republic of Ireland defender, who discusses the highs and lows of his career

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Tommy Martin catches up with former Premier League referee Dermot Gallagher. Dermot tells his story from growing up in Ringsend to becoming one of the most recognisable officials in Englands top flight

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories, looking back at the headlines from 1998

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: A look back at the stories making the headlines in 1999, set to a soundtrack of music from the year

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host as Natalie Cassidy, Jack Osbourne, Jordan North, Shaun Wright-Phillips, Hugh Dennis and Kelly Hoppen take on one another in the shows first-ever six-way cooking battle. The contestants have to deliver a British classic using only air fryers in just 30 minutes, with the bottom two having to cook more speedy dishes in a bid to secure a place in the quarter-final

programme.title: Animal Emergency
programme.desc: Clare native Vincent is attending the animal hospital with Porsche, his once speedy greyhound that he fears is on her last legs. Meanwhile, making the trip from Wicklow is Kealan Doyle and his pet Kunekune pigs Hank and Joey, who are lined up for a badly needed operation. Sapphire, a Shibu Inu dog is brought to the Vet Hospital after a sudden decline in her health, but her medical needs come at a huge financial cost for owner Bernadette

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: A lively mix of celebrity chat with national and international guests, fabulous food from the country?s top Chefs, expert advice and all the unmissable stories making the headlines

programme.title: Inside Sydney Airport
programme.desc: The work never slows at Sydney Airport, whether its changing a wind sock, painting a runway at midnight or putting out a potentially catastrophic fire

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the former Crystal Palace and Republic of Ireland defender, who discusses the highs and lows of his career

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Irelands most decorated cerebral palsy footballer talks to Tommy Martin about his successful career and the personal challenges he overcame

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the jockey, who reflects on a pioneering career, a life immersed in horse racing, and her role as a role model to other women

programme.title: Whos Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Comedian Vic Reeves and his wife Nancy Sorrell put on a dinner party as they play host to four strangers, who have to try and work out their identities by solving cryptic clues. Hosted by Brian McFadden

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: A bloody attack on a young swimmer points to something dangerous in a remote stretch of the Parana River in Argentina. Jeremy Wade discovers locals are blaming a creature they call the River Tiger - a muscular predator with jaws like a bolt cutter - but to back up their stories, the freshwater detective needs to catch a specimen big enough to inflict the chilling wounds he has heard about

programme.title: Paul OGrady for the Love of Dogs: What Happened Next
programme.desc: The comedian catches up with some of the most memorable dogs he has met from the eight years he has spent at Battersea Dogs and Cats Home. In the third episode of the series, Paul has a heart-warming reunion with Whisper, the most anxious dog he met during his time at Battersea, and finds out how a puppy born with a missing paw is enjoying its new life. Plus, Paul catches up with Cookie, the Staffie who now works as a prison sniffer dog, and Max, an obese dog who needed a diet as well as a new home

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as contestants from across the UK take on Chasers Darragh Ennis, Mark Labbett, Jenny Ryan, Issa Schultz and Shaun Wallace, hoping to win big money

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Relationship-based reality show in which singletons seeking a romance compete in the hope of finding love - and winning a £50,000 prize

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: The Tonight Show
programme.desc: Comment and analysis on the big stories of the day in Ireland and abroad with a panel of guests covering the key issues

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Mr Middleditch makes a surprise return to the hospital in the back of an ambulance, having hurt his ankle while fishing. Stella grudgingly welcomes Marian McKaig as her room-mate, but the new nurse quickly catches Aluns eye. Meanwhile, a frantic mother calls for Mikes help at a hippie commune, where her hostile husband is refusing help from the outside world. Ian Carmichael rejoins the series, which continues from earlier in the year. With Ted Robbins

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the former football player and manager, who talks to Tommy Martin about his life as well as his sporting career

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The boxer talks about his life inside and outside the ring. From growing up as a triplet to when he became the first Irishman to win an Olympic Gold medal in boxing in 1992

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories, looking back at the headline stories from 2000 to a soundtrack of contemporary music

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories from 2001, the year when two planes hit the Twin Towers in New York. It also saw the foot-and-mouth outbreak and Eamon Dunphy turn his hand to TV presenting

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host as Natalie Cassidy, Jack Osbourne and Kelly Hoppen try to impress with family favourite dishes in a three-way cooking battle. The second contest sees Jordan North go up against Shaun Wright-Phillips in a bid to secure a place in the semi-final, with the stars who fail to impress heading into the dreaded cook-off

programme.title: Animal Emergency
programme.desc: Labrador pup Bonnie must undergo life-saving heart surgery, while ragdoll cat owner John needs to uncover what is wrong with his favourite feline

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: A lively mix of celebrity chat with national and international guests, fabulous food from the country?s top Chefs, expert advice and all the unmissable stories making the headlines

programme.title: Inside Sydney Airport
programme.desc: The airport is thrown into jeopardy during a busy Christmas period with the discovery of an unidentified white powder on board an incoming flight

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the former football player and manager, who talks to Tommy Martin about his life as well as his sporting career

programme.title: Alan Quinlan: Red Blooded
programme.desc: Part one of two. Alan Quinlan talks to Joe Molloy about his Ireland career. The back-row forward won 27 international caps during a nine-year period full of ups and downs. In the first part, he discusses heartbreak in 2003, when dislocated his shoulder in the process of scoring against Argentina, an injury which ruled him out of the World Cup in Australia

programme.title: Whos Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: DJ Mike Read puts on a dinner party at his Oxfordshire home for four dinner guests as he plays mystery celebrity host. Will they need to take host Brian McFaddens Poptastic bonus to guess Reads identity?

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade continues his investigations into supposedly deadly freshwater creatures. From right across the Amazon there are stories of a colossal fish that is said to swallow men whole. Some call it the lau-lau, others the piraiba. But after 20 years of criss-crossing South America, Jeremy has never seen a specimen big enough to convince him that the fishermens tales are true. In this programme, he heads to a remote and little-known backwater of Guyana on a mission to finally track down this alleged monster

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Binesh from Bristol, Tash from Liverpool, Omar from Newcastle-under-Lyme and Annabelle from Burgess Hill pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.desc: A recent life-changing medical diagnosis has thrown psychologist Joe OLoughlin off-balance, and he is feeling reckless. When a young woman is found murdered, the unusual manner of her death prompts DS Riya Devi to suggest asking Joe for a professional opinion. Joe is shocked by the sight of the body - but is he just shaken by the brutality of the killing or is there something from his past hes not telling the police? Thriller, starring Aidan Turner, Shaun Parkes and Anjli Mohindra

programme.title: Are You Autistic?
programme.desc: A documentary challenging what people think they know about autism, exploring myths surrounding the condition and examining what living with autism is really like. Anna Richardson, whose nephew has recently been diagnosed, and campaigners Georgia Harper and Sam Ahern, who are both autistic, set out to uncover the true face of autism, working closely with charities, experts and people from across the spectrum. In a unique experiment, a team of leading experts have agreed to adapt their research exclusively for the programme, and test two adults who think they are in need of a diagnosis

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: The detective and his team are called to the site of a fatal crash involving an articulated lorry, a car and a blue pick-up truck. programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the murders of Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman in 2002, detailing events of the desperate 14-day search by Cambridgeshire Police to the eventual discovery of their bodies

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: The Ipcress File
programme.desc: In 1962 Berlin, British Army corporal Harry Palmer is released from military prison at the behest of unorthodox spymaster Dalby, who wants him to help find an abducted scientist critical to the creation of a new bomb to scare the Soviet Union. Cold War spy thriller adapted by John Hodge from the Len Deighton novel, starring Joe Cole, with Lucy Boynton and Tom Hollander

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Judge Rinder rules the courtroom in his hit series, but now the respected barrister with a criminal defence background steps out from behind the bench to take on real cases.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Phil and Geoff keep watch on the illegal gambling club owned by Kenny Dekin (guest Ron Donachie), in the hope of finding evidence to close the establishment. Rob is concerned about his future with Carol as he anxiously awaits the results of his sergeant exams, while David is pleased with the outcome when he strikes out on his own

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: As the Merriwicks delve into the images on Joys vision board, Cassie accompanies Sam on a trip to his childhood home to care for his sister. Catherine Bell stars

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: An insight into a team of engineers in Perths attempts to not only break the 763mph land speed record set back in 1997, but also go past the 1,000mph barrier

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: The friends go all out to make Susies birthday a night to remember, and as the celebration begin, Britt is keen to show she still has what it takes by showing off her singing and dancing talents. The senior citizens also head to Chennai, where Zandra is reunited with a friend from 40 years ago and Henry relives some difficult memories of his last overseas broadcast at the citys famous cricket ground. Back in Puducherry, John and Duncan test their fitness by jumping on their bikes, while Paul, revelling in the chaos of the Indian roads, decides to take his mock Indian driving test

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade monster cases usually involve freshwater culprits, after hearing multiple reports of a deep-water serial killer in Mexicos Sea of Cortez, he decides to step out of his comfort zone and into unchartered depths. The extreme angler meets an attack survivor whose chilling account identifies the underwater assassin as el diablo rojo - the red devil, prompting Jeremy to brave sea-sickening conditions to track down a horrific beast, apparently even more fearsome than sharks

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Nuala spends the morning pregnancy-testing the livestock on her family farm, where her father and brother tend 200 dairy cows. Jennie attends Minnie the Jack Russell, who has delivered one of her pups, with more still to come. However, Minnies labour has slowed dangerously and Jennie has to act fast if she is to save the lives of the rest of the litter - leading to a difficult decision for owner Jamie. Meanwhile, an emergency case leaves Darren delivering two sets of twins

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick investigates a break-in at the surgery, while Kate discovers the cause of a patients miscarriages and Greengrass claims his car has been stolen. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.desc: Cameras follow Stefanie and Jade from Harrow who have 10 days to slim down before the launch of their clothing label. There is also advice for fast food junkies Matt from Redditch and Robb from the Wirral. They battle the bulge before a university reunion in six weeks time. Dr Xand van Tulleken and dietician Hala El-Shafie also follow the progress of Vicki and Kerry from Chesterfield, who want to get fighting fit for a mud race in four months

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Best friends Neil and John took over the Vidella Hotel in Blackpool a decade ago, but their love of practical jokes is beginning to wear thin and falling trade has started to take its toll on them. Alex Polizzi tries to turn their struggling business around, but has her work cut out to prove to the owners that a failing hotel is not a laughing matter

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories, looking back at the headline stories from 2000 to a soundtrack of contemporary music

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past 20 years, focusing this week on 2011

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A patient who set fire to his own shop (guest star Tony Slattery) attempts to avoid police questioning by leaping into an ambulance and driving off towards the pier - unaware Alun and a sick man are in the back of the vehicle. Meanwhile, Dr Makori fears for her relationship with Dr Fox when he tells her he has been offered a job in Biafra. Medical drama, with Andy Wear, Paul Fox and Kananu Kirimi

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick awaits the arrival in Aidensfield of a violent criminal recently released from prison, and Kate finds herself cut off by snow when she tries to help a heavily pregnant patient get to hospital. Meanwhile, Greengrass braves the harsh weather himself when his faithful canine companion Alfred goes missing. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The death of an ancient estates patriarch leads to rivalry and murder when his rebellious son plans to sell it off, outraging his siblings. Detective drama set in a picturesque rural county where death lurks around every corner

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: A vengeful Maggie pays Megan a visit at her flat, Lou comes to the rescue when Debbie picks a fight, George despairs as Adam and Alya build the case against him, and Bethany confides in Jodie about being stood up

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain feels he has been betrayed, while Joe is annoyed and Marlon is put out

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: Harry is shipped back to Manchester due to concerns about his mental health, and is furious at the implication he is doomed to follow in his fathers footsteps. As he returns home, Kasia prepares to leave for Europe on an important mission. Henriette returns home have smuggled David out of France, and Marga plots to flee for Berlin. Rajib encounters a German patrol and tries to negotiate a ceasefire

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.sub-title: Nicolla Fellow and Karen Hadaway and Oporation Sevent
programme.desc: Judge Rinder rules the courtroom in his hit series, but now the respected barrister with a criminal defence background steps out from behind the bench to take on real cases

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Intrigue spreads among the residents of Aidensfield as Frances Elliot causes havoc with her car. An outraged Peggy decides to investigate the rumours about the newcomer and discovers she appears to be hiding something. Rob receives some good news in the post but is later forced into making a life-changing decision. Kerrie Taylor guests

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Cassie and Sam are disagreeing about Sams return to work after his injury. Flower Universe is trying to undercut Abigails business, but she wont go down without a fight

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look onboard the $200million Black Pearl, which is the largest sailing yacht in the world and has been built to sail faster than any ship of its size

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Saying goodbye to Puducherry, the celebrities journey north to the foothills of the Himalayas for their final week in India. Following a treacherous drive up narrow, winding roads, they arrive at their new home on the outskirts of Rishikesh, ready to experience the spiritual side of life in Northern India. After starting the next day with an outdoor group meditation session among the wild monkeys and spectacular views, Duncan and John waste no time exploring the bustling town of Rishikesh, which attracts thousands of pilgrims from all over the world

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: The island of Andros in the West Indies boasts tales of swimmers being ripped from the surface of the islands mysterious blue holes by a monster known as the Lusca. Described as half-shark, half-octopus with a taste for human flesh, it is said to drag its prey a thousand feet down to its deep water lair. Extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to the tropical paradise, with his quest for answers taking him to dangerous new depths and closer than ever to a real killer

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: Drama about the lives of a group of Womens Institute members in a rural Cheshire community during the Second World War. In the face of an impending conflict with Germany, the Great Paxford WI faces a difficult decision. When president Joyce Cameron resigns, taking most of the membership with her, Frances Barden and her supporters must recruit new helpers from unexpected places. Starring Samantha Bond, Francesca Annis, Ruth Gemmell, Claire Rushbrook and Frances Grey, with Ed Stoppard and Mark Bazeley

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick awaits the arrival in Aidensfield of a violent criminal recently released from prison, and Kate finds herself cut off by snow when she tries to help a heavily pregnant patient get to hospital. Meanwhile, Greengrass braves the harsh weather himself when his faithful canine companion Alfred goes missing. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.desc: Dr Xand van Tulleken and dietician Hala El-Shafie help Amber lose weight for her graduation day, with the aid of the mono meal. Cameras also follow Roisin is on the Dukan diet of lean protein, oat bran and water, and Gerry and Leighton, who are on six week plans for Leightons wedding. Plus, Janet and Carol have four months to test out the DNA diet and the apples and pears weight loss regime. Theres also a look at vitamin drips and placenta pills

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Church minister John Humberstone has been welcoming guests to the Grade-II listed Alexandra Hotel in Llandudno for the past couple of years, but the business is in deep trouble. Alex Polizzi heads to north Wales to try to revive the establishments fortunes, but has her work cut out to transform its failing restaurant, bad reputation and negative reviews

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain feels he has been betrayed, while Joe is annoyed and Marlon is put out

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: A vengeful Maggie pays Megan a visit at her flat, Lou comes to the rescue when Debbie picks a fight, George despairs as Adam and Alya build the case against him, and Bethany confides in Jodie about being stood up

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A wedding reception held on Elsinby Pier ends in tragedy when a fishing trawler sails badly off course. Meanwhile, Adam faces up to a tough decision and the staff at the hospital prepare for a new arrival as Jill goes into labour. Robert Cavanah and Amy Robbins star

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A womans body is pulled from the river, reminding Frost that he investigated the death of her husbands first wife years ago. Unfortunately, it turns out that a key witnesss statement went astray during the earlier case - which leads to Frost being suspended from duty. Discreetly continuing his enquiries, he is intrigued to learn the local priest recently heard a very disturbing confession. Starring David Jason, Eoin McCarthy, Anthony Calf, Kenneth Cope and Isla Blair

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick faces a dilemma after one of his colleagues is suspected of robbery and Greengrass develops a keen interest in a villagers greyhound. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: North Shore
programme.desc: The death of 19-year-old Sophie Chalcott, the daughter of the UK minister for trade, sparks international interest after her body is found floating in Sydney harbour the morning after a lavish party on a boat. British detective Max Drummond arrives in Sydney to find a complex homicide investigation under way and clashes immediately with DS Meg Driscoll on her first murder case as lead detective. Thriller, starring John Bradley, Joanne Froggatt and Kirsty Sturgess

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maggie faces a grilling for assaulting Megan, Todd thinks on his feet when Theo discovers his escape bag, Ken grows concerned at Daniels bitterness, and Bethany queries Jodies credentials

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy reaches his breaking point, while Jacob is determined and Marlon helps one of his friends

programme.title: Spies of Terror
programme.desc: After a terrorism attack in Paris, government agents race to catch those responsible. Spy thriller, starring Rachida Brakni, Fleur Geffrier, Vincent Elbaz and Pierre Perrier. In French

programme.title: Dublin After Dark
programme.desc: Efforts of members of An Garda Síochána to combat growing levels of anti-social behaviours on the streets of the capital. Garda Shauna Naughton and Garda Jun Han monitor the streets of Dublin on their nightly patrols in some of the worst hot spots for crime

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A gang of bikers wreaks havoc in Aidensfield, prompting the locals to demand a new bobby. When young recruit PC Joe Mason (Joe McFadden) arrives he is not quite what they expected, with his tactics for crime fighting making his reception a mixed one between both residents and fellow police officers

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: The pressure is on when strange things start happening to Cassie, Abigail and Joys abilities. Fantasy, starring Catherine Bell

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look at the Qinghai-Tibet line, the highest railway in the world, providing an insight into the engineering challenges faced at high altitude and in bitterly cold temperatures

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Documentary following life at the Spanish Rio Park hotel and the holiday-makers to whom they cater. The first edition sees the staff ready to welcome thousands of Brits, but no sooner have they started than there is a problem when the lifts break down and they race to get them repaired

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler and underwater detective Jeremy Wade goes in search of the real creature behind the enduring legend of the sea serpent - starting with a violent attack in the English Channel, and culminating in the dark depths of the Mediterranean Sea

programme.title: Tell Me Everything
programme.desc: Jonny is energised and on top of the world, but an exam triggers a setback he didnt see coming. Elsewhere, Ellas wild behaviour at Dylans 18th birthday party alarms her friends.

programme.title: Piglets
programme.desc: Difficult situations must be defused to prevent escalation, while one of the recruits might be expelled if a fellow student reports their misconduct, and theres an unexpected staff sacking. Comedy set in a police training college, starring Sarah Parish and Mark Heap

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants David, Raj, Jo and Sophy vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot
